name: serve
description: Serves local directories securely using SSL/HTTPS certificates in a lightweight background process. Logs HTTP access automatically to ~/.pi-certs/logs/. Available as the /serve command inside Pi Coding Agent, or as the standalone ./serve (or bare serve, once the repo root is on $PATH) CLI script from any other shell, including Claude Code's '!' prefix.

examples.desc: Serve public/ on 8080 and docs/ on 8081 (the defaults).
Serve public/ and docs/ statically, with hot-reloading disabled.
Serve src/ on 8080 and assets/ on 8081.
Serve a directory containing a .env file, bypassing the warning (CLI only).
Kill the server on port 8080 and the server serving docs/.
Instantly terminate every active dev server across all worktrees.

usage.desc: Serve specified directories (default: public, docs) on HTTPS ports.
Disable live reloading and serve pure static files without code injection or file watching.
Bypass the .env secret warning and serve the directory anyway. CLI only — Pi prompts interactively instead.
Discover and display the active servers list. Pi only — no persistent widget in the CLI.
Hide the active servers list from the screen. Pi only.
Disable emoji icons and use clean single-width ASCII characters for TUI widgets.
Enable emoji icons in TUI widgets.
List all active servers serving directories within this worktree.
Kill servers by port, directory, or 'all'. Omit the target to kill every server in this worktree.
